meta.title: The Cow and the Tiger — Vocabulary Set 3
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Week 7 vocabulary focus: promise, attack, agreed, grassy, surprised, happy, protect, truth.

questions.question: What does 'promise' mean?
options: To say you will definitely do something
questions.explanation: A 'promise' is when you say you will definitely do something. When you make a promise, you give your word — it is a serious commitment.

questions.question: A 'grassy' field has lots of green grass growing on it.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Grassy' means covered with grass. A grassy field is a large open area where green grass grows — perfect for cows to graze.

questions.question: It is important to tell the _______ — even when it is difficult.
questions.hint: The real facts; what really happened
questions.explanation: 'Truth' means what is real and correct — not a lie. Telling the truth means saying what really happened, even if it is hard.

questions.question: What does 'protect' mean?
options: To keep someone safe from danger
questions.explanation: 'Protect' means to keep safe from harm or danger. The farmer wanted to protect Chumpa from the tiger — to make sure she would not be attacked.

questions.question: The dog will _______ if it feels scared or angry.
questions.hint: To start fighting; to hurt someone
questions.explanation: 'Attack' means to start fighting or hurting someone. When an animal feels threatened, it may attack — it fights to protect itself.

questions.question: They _______ on the rules before the game started. Everyone said yes.
questions.explanation: 'Agreed' means said yes to something. When everyone agreed on the rules, they all accepted them and said yes to playing by them.

questions.question: I was _______ when my friend gave me a gift — I did not expect it at all!
questions.explanation: 'Surprised' means feeling shocked by something you did not expect. An unexpected gift makes you feel surprised — you did not know it was coming.

questions.question: 'Agreed' means the same as 'said yes' or 'accepted.'
questions.explanation: Yes! When you 'agree,' you accept a plan or idea and say yes to it. The tiger 'agreed' to the farmer's plan — he said yes and accepted the deal.

questions.question: Sort these Week 7 words — are they FEELINGS or ACTIONS?
questions.explanation: 'Surprised' (emotion) and 'happy' (emotion) are feelings. 'Truth' is a thing you can feel strongly about. 'Attack,' 'protect,' and 'agreed' are actions — things you do.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The tiger promised not to attack Chumpa.' The tiger gave his word — his promise — that he would not harm the cow.

questions.question: She was _______ when she heard the news — her face broke into a big smile.
questions.explanation: 'Happy' means feeling glad and joyful. Smiling when you hear good news shows you are happy — you feel positive and pleased.

questions.question: The opposite of 'truth' is:
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'truth' (what is real) is 'a lie' or 'a falsehood' (something that is not true). The tiger broke his promise — which was the opposite of telling the truth.

questions.question: A 'promise' and an 'agreement' are different things — a promise is personal, while an agreement involves two or more people.
questions.explanation: Yes! A 'promise' is when ONE person commits to doing something. An 'agreement' usually involves TWO or more people both saying yes. They are related but slightly different.

questions.question: Which sentence uses BOTH 'protect' and 'attack' correctly?
options: The farmer attacked to protect a tiger.
She attacked her umbrella to protect from the rain.
A helmet is worn to protect your head in case you fall or are attacked.
questions.explanation: A helmet 'protects' your head by keeping it safe. In a contact sport, you might be 'attacked' (hit). Both words are used correctly: protect = keep safe, attack = a hit or strike.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence about the story's moral:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Always tell the truth even when it is hard.' This is the moral lesson of the story — honesty matters, even when it requires courage.
